log_sources.name: syscab_install_log
log_sources.description: Log of syscab install. Could provide information for remote node shutdown/unreachable.

log_sources.name: syscab_diag_log
log_sources.description: Log of syscab diag. Could provide information for remote node hardware problem (network, RAID, etc).

log_sources.name: ge_sys_log
log_sources.description: Log of system level. High-level problems of all nodes will be gathered in here. Here the gesys_*.log is a match pattern, use ls -l to find the actual name at first.

tools.name: check_disk
tools.when_to_use: when you suspect that ths disk is low
tools.notes: If the disk space is low, you should give a warning

tools.name: check_process
tools.when_to_use: Check if the target process is running
tools.notes: Combine with grep to filter the process name

tools.name: check_service_status
tools.when_to_use: Check if the systemd service is running as expected

tools.name: check_service_status
tools.description: Check systemd service on remote nodes
tools.when_to_use: Check if the systemd service is running as expected
tools.notes: Some important services are managed by systemd on sdac, such as raw-data-service, ge_cpuset and so on

tools.name: igc1_check_process
tools.when_to_use: Need to check if the igc1 works well
tools.notes: Use ssh to login igc1, like ssh igc1 ps -ef | grep obelus. Other nodes are the same.

tools.name: Check CT App Up/Down
tools.description: Show the status of all processes of CT App
tools.when_to_use: When user complains that the CT App is not available
tools.nodes: A very simple command to check if the CT App is up or down. Focus on the percent in the output, if it is not 100%, it means there is something wrong with the CT APP

tools.name: Shutdown CT App
tools.when_to_use: If any command need to run with App down, use cleanMon first to make sure that the App is down.
tools.notes: If cleanMon is used once, no need to run again in followed steps. Unless you use st to start the App.

tools.name: Start CT App
tools.when_to_use: If any command need to run with App up, use st first to make sure that the App is up.
tools.notes: If some processes of the CT App fail to start, it may cause the entire App to shutdown.

tools.name: syscab_diag
tools.when_to_use: When there is something wrong with remote nodes
tools.notes: Provides the hardware info of remote nodes. Run syscab diag will diag all nodes. If you want to diagnostic specific node, use syscab diag <node_name>

tools.name: syscab_option
tools.description: Check options installed on remote nodes
tools.when_to_use: Remote node is loaded successfully but cannot work properly
tools.notes: In remote nodes we only care about XtreamRecon and HyperResolution options. XtreamRecon enables node: igc2. HyperResolution enables node: xig1.

tools.name: syscab_query
tools.description: Check the hardware config of remote nodes
tools.when_to_use: Need to confirm the hardware type of remote nodes
tools.notes: The result of this command is a 7-digital part number like 6765000. In general, 6765000 is refered as G5, 6764000 is refered as G3, 6764000-2 is refered as G4 and 6765000-w6164299 is refered as Hyper

tools.name: stop_cup_process
tools.when_to_use: Stop a cup process and do some check
tools.notes: This command will stop a cup process and may cause the entire CT App to shutdown

tools.name: restart_cup_process
tools.when_to_use: When try to restart a cup process to see if this can fix something
tools.notes: If you want to restart recon, use RECON_RESTART

tools.name: Recon stop
tools.description: Shutdown the ig_watchdog and the obelus processes on remote nodes
tools.when_to_use: Need to stop the obelus processes and check something
